nodes.label: Astro Background Development Server
nodes.rationale: Enforces starting the dev server in background mode with astro dev --background and managing it with astro dev stop/status/logs.

nodes.label: Claude Astro Background Server Setup
nodes.rationale: Directs background server startup and control instructions identically to AGENTS.md.

nodes.label: Floating Grid Concept
nodes.rationale: Enforces that all sections must breathe. Use layout background gradients rather than structural boxes to separate blocks. Double padding between core elements.

nodes.label: Vazirmatn Multilingual Styling
nodes.rationale: Requires Vazirmatn font family with leading-relaxed line height for Persian/Farsi linguistic text to ensure typographic balance.

nodes.label: Tailwind Light/Dark Mode Base
nodes.rationale: Implements light/dark toggle using Tailwind class mechanism. Base colors are bg-slate-950 (dark mode) and bg-zinc-50/bg-slate-50 (light mode).

nodes.label: Banner Composition Rules
nodes.rationale: Sets 3-Zone Rule for visual hierarchy, Safe Zones (central 70-80%), and CTA requirements (high contrast, bottom-right terminal placement, min 44px height).

nodes.label: Asset Naming Convention
nodes.rationale: Standardizes naming using format {type}_{campaign}_{description}_{timestamp}_{variant}.{ext} with lowercase type and kebab-case campaign/description.

nodes.label: Color Hierarchy & Compliance Ratios
nodes.rationale: Defines 60/30/10 ratio for Primary/Secondary/Accent colors, WCAG 2.1 contrast ratios (4.5:1 for normal text), and max 20% off-brand tolerance.

nodes.label: Logo Clear Space Rule
nodes.rationale: Requires clear space around the logo to equal the height of the logo icon mark [x] on all sides.

nodes.label: Type Scale & Line Height Rules
nodes.rationale: Sets base size of 16px, scale ratio of 1.25 (Major Third), maximum line length of 65-75 characters, and tighter display line heights (1.1-1.3).

nodes.label: Voice Dimensions & Adaptation
nodes.rationale: Maps brand voice on four dimensions: Formal-Casual, Simple-Complex, Serious-Playful, and Reserved-Expressive, adapting tone to context.

nodes.label: Three-Layer Token Architecture
nodes.rationale: Structures design tokens in three layers: Primitive (raw values) -> Semantic (purpose aliases) -> Component (specific overrides) to ensure theming flexibility.

nodes.label: Strategic HTML Slide System
nodes.rationale: Implements pattern breaking Duarte Sparkline (What Is vs What Could Be) at 1/3 and 2/3 beats, and enforces that slides must import design-tokens.css.

nodes.label: Banner Sizes and Art Direction Styles Reference
nodes.norm_label: banner sizes and art direction styles reference

nodes.label: Corporate Identity Program Prompt Engineering Guide
nodes.norm_label: corporate identity program prompt engineering guide

nodes.label: Corporate Identity Program Design Style Guide
nodes.norm_label: corporate identity program design style guide

nodes.label: Copywriting Formula to Slide Type Mapping
nodes.norm_label: copywriting formula to slide type mapping

nodes.label: Social Photos Design and Rendering Guide
nodes.norm_label: social photos design and rendering guide

nodes.label: Slides Copywriting Formulas Reference in Slides Skill
nodes.norm_label: slides copywriting formulas reference in slides skill

nodes.label: Advanced Full-Text Search
nodes.rationale: LIKE with wildcards cannot use indexes and requires full table scans. Using tsvector for full-text search is 100x faster and supports ranking.

nodes.label: Advanced JSONB Indexing
nodes.rationale: JSONB queries without indexes scan the entire table. Using GIN indexes for containment queries or expression indexes for specific keys increases query performance by 10-100x.

nodes.label: Configure Idle Connection Timeouts
nodes.rationale: Idle connections waste resources and hold locks indefinitely. Timeouts like idle_in_transaction_session_timeout or idle_session_timeout reclaim 30-50% of connection slots.

nodes.label: Set Appropriate Connection Limits
nodes.rationale: Too many connections exhaust database memory and degrade performance. Calculate max_connections based on available RAM and reserve resources appropriately.

nodes.label: Use Connection Pooling
nodes.rationale: Postgres connections are expensive (1-3MB RAM each). Without pooling, applications exhaust connections under load. Reusing a small pool allows handling 10-100x more concurrent users.

nodes.label: Use Prepared Statements Correctly with Pooling
nodes.rationale: Prepared statements are tied to individual connections. In transaction-mode pooling, connections are shared, causing errors. Solution is to use unnamed statements, deallocate after use, or use session mode.
nodes.norm_label: use prepared statements correctly with pooling

nodes.label: Batch INSERT Statements for Bulk Data
nodes.rationale: Individual INSERT statements have high overhead due to round trips and transaction commits. Batching multiple rows or using COPY yields 10-50x faster bulk inserts.
nodes.norm_label: batch insert statements for bulk data

nodes.label: Eliminate N+1 Queries with Batch Loading
nodes.rationale: Executing individual queries in a loop creates significant network overhead. Batching queries into a single query using arrays or JOINs reduces database round trips by 10-100x.
nodes.norm_label: eliminate n+1 queries with batch loading

nodes.label: Use Cursor-Based Pagination Instead of OFFSET
nodes.rationale: OFFSET-based pagination scans all skipped rows, becoming slower on deeper pages. Keyset/cursor pagination uses indexes for consistent O(1) performance regardless of page depth.
nodes.norm_label: use cursor-based pagination instead of offset

nodes.label: Use UPSERT for Insert-or-Update Operations
nodes.rationale: Checking existence and then inserting/updating creates race conditions in concurrent environments. INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ensures atomic operations.

nodes.label: Use Advisory Locks for Application-Level Locking
nodes.rationale: Creating database rows simply for locking overhead is inefficient. Advisory locks provide lightweight application-level coordination without row-level lock overhead.
nodes.norm_label: use advisory locks for application-level locking

nodes.label: Prevent Deadlocks with Consistent Lock Ordering
nodes.rationale: Deadlocks occur when transactions lock resources in different orders. Locking resources consistently in order or utilizing single-statement atomic updates prevents deadlock errors.
nodes.norm_label: prevent deadlocks with consistent lock ordering

nodes.label: Keep Transactions Short to Reduce Lock Contention
nodes.rationale: Long-running transactions hold locks that block other queries. Performing external operations (like API calls) outside transaction scope and keeping transaction logic minimal improves throughput 3-5x.
nodes.norm_label: keep transactions short to reduce lock contention

nodes.label: Nancy Duarte Sparkline Pattern
nodes.rationale: Alternates between current pain ('What Is') and a better future ('What Could Be') to maintain engagement, with pattern breaks at 1/3 and 2/3 positions.

nodes.label: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
nodes.rationale: Using SKIP LOCKED allows concurrent worker queues to fetch and lock next available rows in parallel without blocking each other, increasing queue processing throughput by up to 10x.

nodes.label: Query Execution Profiling
nodes.rationale: Executing EXPLAIN ANALYZE provides the actual runtime execution plan, showing physical operator timings, row estimations vs reality, and buffer read/write metrics to diagnose query bottlenecks.

nodes.label: Database Vacuuming and Statistics Maintenance
nodes.rationale: Regular autovacuuming reclaims dead tuples, while ANALYZE updates query planner statistics. Stale statistics cause Postgres to choose poor query plans, e.g., Seq Scans instead of Index Scans.

nodes.label: Composite Indexes
nodes.rationale: Indexing multiple columns is more efficient than separate indexes which require bitmap index scans. Leftmost columns should be equality fields and range columns last.

nodes.label: Covering Indexes (INCLUDE)
nodes.rationale: Using the INCLUDE clause stores non-search key payload values in the index leaf, enabling Index-Only Scans and eliminating expensive heap table fetches.

nodes.label: Partial Indexes
nodes.rationale: Index size and write overhead are reduced by indexing only rows that match a WHERE clause predicate, optimizing queries targeting specific subsets (e.g. status='pending').

nodes.label: Idempotent Constraint Migrations
nodes.rationale: PostgreSQL lacks ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS, which causes migrations to fail. Standard practice utilizes SQL DO blocks checking pg_constraint to safely perform ALTER TABLE constraint statements.

nodes.label: Bigint Identity Column Strategy
nodes.rationale: Using bigint generated always as identity instead of serial enforces SQL standard compliance and prevents 2.1 billion overflow limits for high-scale primary keys.

nodes.label: Foreign Key Indexing
nodes.rationale: Foreign key columns are not indexed by default. Creating indexes on them avoids seq scans on parent table updates, deletions (cascade), and joins.

nodes.label: Lowercase snake_case Identifiers
nodes.rationale: Unquoted identifiers fold to lowercase in PostgreSQL. Avoiding quoted mixed-case (camelCase) prevents errors in ORMs, migration frameworks, and AI code generation tools.

nodes.label: Table Partitioning by Range
nodes.rationale: For tables exceeding 100M rows, splitting by time/range confines query sequential scans to relevant subsets and lets operators drop old partitions instantly without massive DELETE operations.

nodes.label: UUIDv7 Primary Key Strategy
nodes.rationale: Random UUIDv4 indexes fragment on high write rates due to scattered leaf insertions. Time-ordered UUIDv7 (or ULID) ensures sequential leaf page writes, preserving index locality.

nodes.label: Principle of Least Privilege for Roles
nodes.rationale: Never use a superuser role. Separate database roles into readonly and writer, granting only minimal select, insert, or update permissions per schema/table to limit SQL injection impact.
nodes.norm_label: principle of least privilege for roles

nodes.label: Row Level Security (RLS)
nodes.rationale: Enforces multi-tenant isolation policies directly at the database engine layer. Restricting queries automatically based on current tenant context prevents user data leaks.

nodes.label: Optimized RLS Policies
nodes.rationale: RLS checks evaluate per row. Wrapping helper functions in SELECT subqueries (e.g. select auth.uid()) caches outputs and improves throughput 100x+. Use SECURITY DEFINER carefully.

nodes.label: Canvas Design Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Canvas design systems prioritize rich visual communication (90% visuals, 10% text) over standard templates, establishing distinct design movements through space, form, and color.

nodes.label: Keyboard Focus and Motion Management
nodes.rationale: Enforces interactive keyboard accessibility through focus trapping, keyboard-based event routing, high-contrast visual focus rings, and media query overrides for reduced motion.

nodes.label: shadcn/ui CLI
nodes.rationale: Allows adding component source files directly to the codebase for complete customization control rather than importing from a compiled npm package.

nodes.label: Master and Overrides Design System Pattern
nodes.rationale: Allows hierarchical design system retrieval across multiple pages and chat sessions, maintaining a global source of truth (MASTER.md) while permitting page-specific deviations.
nodes.norm_label: master and overrides design system pattern

nodes.label: Mobile-First Design
nodes.rationale: Base styles apply to small viewports first, with progressive enhancement at larger breakpoint thresholds using min-width media queries to minimize layout shifts.

nodes.label: Phosphor Icons Preference
nodes.rationale: Vector-only Phosphor icons are preferred over emojis to ensure consistent rendering, scaling, accessibility, and compatibility with theme design tokens.

nodes.label: Container Queries
nodes.rationale: Enables styling elements based on parent container width rather than device viewport width, facilitating modular component design.

nodes.label: Favicon SVG
nodes.visual_look: Minimalist vector icon depicting a stylized uppercase letter A or triangular emblem.
nodes.geometry: 128x128 viewBox, vector path consisting of a primary outer triangular/A-shaped glyph and a secondary curved/loop glyph underneath.
elements: CSS media query for light/dark theme fill colors
nodes.brand_context: The primary browser favicon/logo for the portfolio website, designed to adapt automatically to the user's system light or dark theme settings for consistent visibility.

nodes.label: Astro Logo
nodes.description: Official logo of the Astro web framework, featuring the lowercase wordmark 'astro' in dark charcoal and a stylized rocket flame graphic with a red-to-purple gradient.
nodes.geometry: Geometric sans-serif styled letters with clean bezier curves. The flame element consists of overlapping paths forming a stylized teardrop/rocket exhaust flare.
nodes.elements: A wordmark spelling 'astro' and a gradient-colored rocket flame icon at the bottom-left of the first letter 'a'.
nodes.brand_context: Used as the visual identity and branding asset for the Astro web framework within the portfolio website.

nodes.label: Background Ambient Glow SVG
nodes.description: An SVG graphic containing two large organic glowing blob shapes with 15.4% opacity. The first shape, located at the bottom-left, uses a linear gradient from bright blue (#3245FF) to orchid purple (#BC52EE). The second shape, located at the top-right, uses a linear gradient from red-coral (#D83333) to bright magenta (#F041FF). Designed for a 1440x1024 desktop canvas to serve as a modern ambient decorative background.

hyperedges.label: UI Styling Design and Component Foundation
